user_story: As a manager, I want Sandlot to load the latest Fantrax snapshot when the app opens so I can start from current league data.
expected_behavior: On mount, the app requests /api/snapshot/latest, normalizes the payload, stores it as the active model, and shows the returned sync state when roster data exists.
test_method: Playwright smoke with mocked and live snapshot responses; API contract test
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

user_story: As a manager, I want a clear no-data or failed-sync state so I know the app cannot yet read Fantrax.
expected_behavior: If /api/snapshot/latest fails or returns an empty roster, the app sets sync state to failed, preserves any fallback snapshot, and surfaces the error or no-data copy.
test_method: Playwright route mock for 404, 503, and empty-roster snapshot
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

user_story: As a manager, I want to refresh Fantrax data manually so I can pull a newer roster and matchup state.
expected_behavior: Refresh buttons POST /api/refresh, send an optional refresh token from localStorage, replace the model with the refreshed snapshot, and show fallback details when refresh fails with a stored snapshot.
test_method: API contract test plus Playwright click through Today and non-Today refresh controls
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

user_story: As a manager, I want fast access to the main app areas from the bottom navigation so I can move between workflows on mobile.
expected_behavior: The bottom bar exposes Today, Roster, Skipper, Adds, and League tabs; clicking a tab switches page state and clears league team drill-in when leaving League.
test_method: Playwright keyboard and pointer navigation smoke
notes: Skipper is intentionally centered in the five-tab bottom nav.

user_story: As a manager, I want the auth screen to collect my email and move into the app during demo mode so the shell can be exercised without real auth wiring.
expected_behavior: When initial auth requires sign-in, the auth screen accepts an email, shows a sent state, and allows entry through the sign-in callback.
test_method: Component/browser test with initial auth flag
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

user_story: As a manager, I want the Today page to summarize urgent roster issues so I can decide what needs action first.
expected_behavior: Today computes roster health and matchup recommendation items, counts urgent/check/review items, and displays an ordered Attention Queue from the latest model.
test_method: Playwright Today test with representative snapshot; unit coverage for attention ordering
notes: Existing related tests: tests/playwright/specs/today-attention.spec.ts and tests/test_sandlot_attention.py.

user_story: As a manager, I want clicking an attention item to open the right player or workflow so I can act immediately.
expected_behavior: A row with a player id opens the player sheet; a row with navigation metadata sends the app to that nav target.
test_method: Playwright click test for player and nav attention rows
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

user_story: As a manager, I want a clear empty state when there are no issues or no data so I understand whether the roster is clean or unavailable.
expected_behavior: The queue renders Clear copy when real data has no items, failed-copy when sync failed, and first-snapshot copy before real data exists.
test_method: Playwright route mocks for clean snapshot, failed sync, and no-data states
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

user_story: As a manager, I want to see current matchup score, opponent, margin, and time left so I know if I am leading or chasing.
expected_behavior: When matchup data exists, Today displays my score, opponent score, leading/trailing/tied state, margin, opponent name, and days left.
test_method: Playwright Today snapshot fixture plus visual assertion
notes: Existing related test: tests/playwright/specs/today.spec.ts.

user_story: As a manager, I want projected final score and win probability when projection data is complete so I can judge risk beyond current score.
expected_behavior: If projection info is complete, Today displays projected score and a win probability ring; if projection data is incomplete, it shows a paused reason instead.
test_method: Playwright projection fixture; unit tests for projection/data-quality logic
notes: Existing related tests: tests/playwright/specs/today-projection.spec.ts and tests/test_sandlot_matchup.py.

user_story: As a manager, I want matchup advice to be withheld clearly when data quality is incomplete so I do not trust bad recommendations.
expected_behavior: If API data says recommendations_ready is false and no matchup recommendations exist, Today shows an Advice paused caution with the quality reason.
test_method: Playwright fixture with recommendations_ready false; data-quality unit test
notes: Existing related test: tests/test_sandlot_recommendations.py.

user_story: As a manager, I want Today to route me directly to waiver review or Skipper so the highest-frequency actions are one tap away.
expected_behavior: Review waiver board switches to Adds; Ask Skipper switches to Skipper.
test_method: Playwright click test from Today to Adds and Skipper
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

user_story: As a manager, I want to filter my roster by starting, bench, or all players so I can inspect the right group quickly.
expected_behavior: The Roster tab filters by slot against inactive/bench constants, shows empty copy when a segment has no players, and keeps all mode unfiltered.
test_method: Playwright roster segment test
notes: Existing related test: tests/playwright/specs/roster.spec.ts.

user_story: As a manager, I want each roster row to show slot, player, team, opponent, status, and production so I can scan roster health.
expected_behavior: Rows render slot state color, avatar, position/team/opponent details, injury or hot labels, FP/G/projection, and versus expectation when available.
test_method: Playwright snapshot visual assertions; unit test for player normalization if added
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

user_story: As a manager, I want tapping a roster player to open the player sheet so I can inspect detail without leaving the app.
expected_behavior: Roster rows call onPlayer with the player's id and V2App renders V2PlayerSheet for the selected detail id.
test_method: Playwright roster-to-player-sheet test
notes: Existing related test: tests/playwright/specs/player-sheet.spec.ts.

user_story: As a manager, I want quick top-player and watch-list signals so I can spot roster context without opening every row.
expected_behavior: Roster computes the highest FP/G or projection player and a watch player based on injury or bench state, then displays both in mini insight cards.
test_method: Playwright text assertions using controlled snapshot
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

user_story: As a manager, I want to sort league standings by rank, points, or name so I can compare teams different ways.
expected_behavior: League renders a segment control for rank/points/name and sorts the leagueTeams model accordingly.
test_method: Playwright sort test on controlled standings fixture
notes: Existing related test: tests/playwright/specs/league.spec.ts.

user_story: As a manager, I want to open another team's roster from standings so I can inspect potential trade targets.
expected_behavior: Clicking a team row stores the team, requests /api/team/{team_id}/roster, and displays that roster with loading, error, empty, segment, and back states.
test_method: Playwright click through standings to team roster; API route contract test
notes: Existing related test: tests/playwright/specs/league.spec.ts.

user_story: As a manager, I want a League entry point into trade grading so I can evaluate offers while browsing teams.
expected_behavior: The Trade desk card opens the hidden Trade page when Grade an offer is clicked.
test_method: Playwright click from League to Trade page
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

user_story: As a manager, I want the Adds page to load ranked waiver swaps and clearly show loading, error, and no-positive-swap states.
expected_behavior: Adds requests /api/waiver-swaps/latest on mount, renders loading and error states, and shows deterministic board copy with card count or API message.
test_method: Playwright route mock for loading, error, empty, and ready states
notes: Existing related test: tests/playwright/specs/adds.spec.ts.

user_story: As a manager, I want to filter waiver cards by position so I can focus on a roster need.
expected_behavior: Adds derives positions from card add positions and filled position, displays horizontal chips, and filters visible cards by selected token.
test_method: Playwright card filter test with multi-position fixture
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

user_story: As a manager, I want each waiver card to show add/drop players, delta, confidence, evidence, reason, risk, and dynasty note so I can judge a move.
expected_behavior: Each waiver swap card renders rank, FP/G delta, confidence, add and move-out players, evidence chips, why/risk/dynasty rationale, and action buttons.
test_method: Playwright ready-board fixture with assertions for card sections
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

user_story: As a manager, I want Review swap to open the add player's profile so I can inspect the free agent before acting.
expected_behavior: The Review swap button calls onOpenPlayer with the add id; V2App routes through openPlayer, switches to Roster page state, and opens the player sheet.
test_method: Playwright Adds card to player sheet test
notes: Existing related test: tests/playwright/specs/adds.spec.ts.

user_story: As a manager, I want the roster-player button to open the move-out player's profile so I can verify who I would drop.
expected_behavior: The Roster player button calls onOpenPlayer with the move_out id when available and is disabled when that id is missing.
test_method: Playwright Adds card move-out button test
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

user_story: As a manager, I want to send a waiver card into Skipper so I can ask follow-up questions with the card context already drafted.
expected_behavior: Continue in Skipper builds a swap-specific prompt, stores it as skipperDraft, switches to Skipper, and fills the chat input from that draft id.
test_method: Playwright Adds to Skipper draft flow
notes: This is part of the critical path named in the loop doc.

user_story: As a manager, I want to see a short waiver brief on Adds when AI warmup succeeded so I can understand the board quickly.
expected_behavior: If the waiver API returns brief.state ready, Adds displays the first two brief lines in a Skipper brief panel.
test_method: Playwright waiver payload with ready brief
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

user_story: As a manager, I want separate pickers for players I give and players I get so I can model an offer.
expected_behavior: The trade page filters playerIndex by source mine for You give and league for You get, supports search by player/team, excludes already selected players, and caps each side at five.
test_method: Playwright picker search/add/remove test; unit test for player index
notes: Existing related tests: tests/playwright/specs/trade.spec.ts and tests/test_sandlot_api_player_index.py.

user_story: As a manager, I want trade grading to avoid accidental empty submissions so incomplete offers are not sent to the grader.
expected_behavior: The trade page only submits when both arrays are non-empty and not loading; the handler returns early when not ready, then POSTs selected ids to /api/trades/grade when ready. Current V2Primary does not propagate the disabled prop, so visual disabled affordance is a Phase 2 UX check.
test_method: Playwright readiness guard and visual affordance test plus API validation test
notes: Existing related tests: tests/playwright/specs/trade.spec.ts and tests/test_sandlot_trades.py.

user_story: As a manager, I want the trade grade result to summarize fairness, grade, deltas, rationale, and model/cache state so I can judge the offer.
expected_behavior: A successful trade grade renders letter grade, fairness ring, your/their/age deltas, weakest position, rationale, cache/model metadata, and no-counter reason when present.
test_method: Playwright mocked grade response; backend unit tests for grade payload
notes: Existing related test: tests/playwright/specs/trade.spec.ts.

user_story: As a manager, I want suggested counters to expand for rationale so I can negotiate from the grade result.
expected_behavior: Result counters render tier, acceptance band, get/give names, FP/G delta, and a collapsible rationale body.
test_method: Playwright mocked grade response with counters and expand click
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

user_story: As a manager, I want Settings to show sync health and let me refresh now so I can manage stale Fantrax data.
expected_behavior: Settings displays healthy/failed sync state, snapshot counts, refresh now button, cookie-backed scraping warning, and app stack details.
test_method: Manual or Playwright state injection for hidden settings page
notes: The Settings page exists in app state but no visible UI entry was found in V2TabBar or top bar.

user_story: As a manager, I want Settings sign out to return me to the auth screen so I can exit the session.
expected_behavior: The Sign out button calls onSignOut, which flips authed false and renders V2Auth.
test_method: Manual or Playwright state injection for hidden settings page
notes: The Settings page exists in app state but no visible UI entry was found in V2TabBar or top bar.

user_story: As a manager, I want player detail to open as an overlay and close without losing my app context.
expected_behavior: When detail id is set, V2PlayerSheet renders over the app, closes on backdrop or close button, and stops propagation inside the sheet.
test_method: Playwright open/close test from roster or Adds
notes: Existing related test: tests/playwright/specs/player-sheet.spec.ts.

feature: Player profile loading and error states
user_story: As a manager, I want player detail to show loading, errors, and retry so I understand profile fetch status.
expected_behavior: The sheet requests /api/player/{fantrax_id}, shows skeleton while loading, shows error copy with retry when no data exists, and preserves data while surfacing later errors.
test_method: Playwright route mocks for loading/error/retry; API route unit test if added
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

user_story: As a manager, I want the Skipper take to fill in when generation finishes so I do not need to close and reopen the player.
expected_behavior: If profile_cache.take is missing or pending and no take text/error exists, the sheet polls /api/player/{id} up to 12 attempts until a take appears or generation stops.
test_method: Playwright mocked sequential player responses with fake timers if practical
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

user_story: As a manager, I want to force a fresh MLB/profile pull for a player so stale detail can be updated on demand.
expected_behavior: The Sync button POSTs /api/player/{id}/refresh, updates data, disables during sync and a cooldown, and surfaces errors without dropping existing data.
test_method: Playwright mocked POST plus disabled/cooldown assertions; API route test if added
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

user_story: As a manager, I want player detail to show ownership, position, age, status, photo, and Skipper take so I can contextualize the player quickly.
expected_behavior: Profile hero displays player photo/avatar, name, team/position/age metadata, owner/source label, injury status, and take text/error/skeleton.
test_method: Playwright profile payload fixture and visual assertions
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

user_story: As a manager, I want headline stats with expandable season and game-log detail so I can inspect recent production.
expected_behavior: Profile stats compute hitting or pitching season aggregates, show L7/L30/headline stat, allow More stats expansion, and show sparkline plus recent game log.
test_method: Playwright expand More stats with hitter and pitcher fixtures; unit tests for stat math if added
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

user_story: As a manager, I want to open media clips from a player sheet so I can inspect highlights or related video.
expected_behavior: Profile clips render media items or placeholders, and opening a clip shows V2ClipViewer with close behavior.
test_method: Playwright click clip row and close viewer
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

user_story: As a manager, I want Skipper to expose available models and reasoning mode so I can choose how the assistant answers.
expected_behavior: Skipper loads /api/skipper/options, normalizes defaults, updates model selector when options differ, and toggles reasoning unless a stream is active.
test_method: Playwright options route mock; unit test for skipper config
notes: Existing related test: tests/test_sandlot_skipper_config.py.

user_story: As a manager, I want Skipper to show the latest waiver refresh brief so chat starts with current context.
expected_behavior: On snapshot change, Skipper requests /api/waiver-swaps/latest, displays up to five brief lines when ready, and shows loading, missing, or error copy otherwise.
test_method: Playwright waiver brief states with route mocks
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

user_story: As a manager, I want previous Skipper messages to load so I can keep context across sessions.
expected_behavior: On mount, Skipper requests /api/skipper/messages, filters broken assistant replies, maps assistant role to ai, and renders history or an error.
test_method: Playwright history route mock; API contract test
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

user_story: As a manager, I want one-tap prompt chips for common questions so I can get useful analysis quickly.
expected_behavior: Skipper renders preset prompt chips; clicking a chip sends that prompt unless streaming is active.
test_method: Playwright click prompt chip and assert stream request body
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

user_story: As a manager, I want Skipper replies to stream into chat so I can see the answer as it is generated.
expected_behavior: Sending non-empty input appends user and ai seed bubbles, POSTs /api/skipper/messages with model/reasoning fields, parses SSE data frames, appends token text, handles stream errors, and clears streaming state.
test_method: Playwright SSE mock; API streaming contract test with TestClient
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

user_story: As a manager, I want deep matchup prompts to include a compact projection card so I can see score context inside the answer.
expected_behavior: Prompts matching deep matchup intent tag the upcoming ai bubble with chart matchup; V2Bubble renders V2MatchupProjectionCard when matchup scores exist.
test_method: Playwright prompt chip or text with matchup fixture
notes: Existing related tests: tests/test_sandlot_skipper_projection.py and tests/test_sandlot_projection_logging.py.

user_story: As a manager, I want player names in Skipper replies to open player sheets so I can jump from advice to details.
expected_behavior: Skipper builds a player name index, renders explicit [[name|id]] tags and fallback full-name matches as V2PlayerLink, and opens player detail through onOpenPlayer.
test_method: Playwright mocked assistant reply containing explicit and fallback player links
notes: Related design doc: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-03-skipper-player-links-design.md.

user_story: As a manager, I want to clear Skipper chat history so I can start over cleanly.
expected_behavior: When messages exist and streaming is inactive, Clear DELETEs /api/skipper/messages, empties local messages, and clears local error state; errors are surfaced in chat.
test_method: Playwright history + clear test; API contract test
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

feature: Web fallback for missing snapshot data
user_story: As a manager, I want Skipper to search public web sources only when snapshot data is missing so I can evaluate players absent from Fantrax data without confusing web facts with league facts.
expected_behavior: Skipper options advertise web-search availability/default state; the UI hides the toggle when unavailable, sends web_search with messages, the backend attaches OpenRouter web_search only when allowed, captures URL citation annotations as first-class sources, and reports actual web-search usage separately from permission.
source_refs: web/sandlot/v2-pages.jsx:V2Skipper; web/sandlot/v2-pages.jsx:V2WebSources; sandlot_api.py:/api/skipper/messages POST; sandlot_api.py:/api/skipper/options; sandlot_skipper.py:SkipperClient.stream
test_method: Unit tests for options/tool/source extraction plus Playwright toggle/source rendering test
notes: External claude -p gate accepted; result captured in docs/quality/second-opinion/skipper-web-fallback-2026-06-21-result.md.

user_story: As an operator, I want /api/health to report database and snapshot freshness so deployments can be checked quickly.
expected_behavior: The endpoint initializes schema, reports database status, latest successful snapshot timestamp/freshness, latest refresh run summary, and safe error text when checks fail.
test_method: API request test against TestClient and Railway smoke
notes: Existing related test: tests/test_sandlot_api_freshness.py.

user_story: As the frontend, I want /api/snapshot/latest to return the normalized app snapshot so UI features share one payload contract.
expected_behavior: The endpoint returns _snapshot_payload for the latest successful snapshot, including roster, standings, matchup projection/recommendations, data quality, player index, freshness, and errors.
test_method: API contract unit test for payload fields and no-snapshot 404
notes: Existing related tests: tests/test_sandlot_data_quality.py and tests/test_sandlot_matchup.py.

user_story: As an external agent, I want /api/attention to return the same actionable queue as Today so automation can inspect needed actions.
expected_behavior: The endpoint reads latest and previous snapshots, returns snapshot ids, freshness, attention items, and status changes; database/no-snapshot failures return 503.
test_method: API contract test locally and Railway deploy check
notes: Existing related tests: tests/test_sandlot_attention.py and tests/playwright/specs/attention-api.spec.ts.

user_story: As an operator or UI, I want /api/refresh to run a manual scrape with safe fallback behavior so stale data can be updated.
expected_behavior: The endpoint enforces optional refresh token, runs refresh, returns skipped fallback if another refresh is running, raises 502 with fallback details on failure, and schedules optional warmups on success.
test_method: API tests for token, skipped fallback, failed fallback, and success warmup behavior
notes: Existing related coverage includes refresh fallback tests in tests/test_sandlot_projection_logging.py.

user_story: As the Adds page and Skipper, I want /api/waiver-swaps/latest to return the latest waiver payload or a clear unavailable status.
expected_behavior: The endpoint returns latest waiver payload, maps missing data to 404, and maps unexpected failures to 503 with logged exception.
test_method: API unit test for ready, missing, and failure paths; Playwright Adds tests
notes: Existing related tests: tests/test_sandlot_waivers.py and tests/test_sandlot_waivers_warm_hash.py.

user_story: As the Trade page, I want /api/trades/grade to validate offer sides and return a grade from the latest snapshot.
expected_behavior: The endpoint requires 1-5 ids per side, returns 409 when no snapshot exists, maps domain grade errors to 400, and returns grade_offer results on success.
test_method: API unit tests for validation, no snapshot, domain error, and success
notes: Existing related test: tests/test_sandlot_trades.py.

user_story: As Skipper, I want options, history, clear, and streaming send endpoints so chat can persist and answer from snapshot context.
expected_behavior: The API exposes model options, persisted history, DELETE clear, and POST streaming SSE that persists user/assistant messages, uses deterministic replies when available, and falls back through model order.
test_method: API streaming/history tests with TestClient and mocked client; frontend SSE test
notes: Existing related tests cover config and projection logging but not full SSE contract.

user_story: As the player sheet, I want player profile GET and refresh POST endpoints so player detail can load and update on demand.
expected_behavior: GET returns player_service profile payload and schedules background refresh when cache or take is missing; POST forces refresh; not found maps to 404 and unexpected errors map to 500.
test_method: API route tests with mocked player_service; Playwright player-sheet tests
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.

user_story: As the League page, I want /api/team/{team_id}/roster to return a team roster from the latest snapshot so I can inspect other teams.
expected_behavior: The endpoint returns team roster rows and capacity metadata from all_team_rosters, with database 503, no-snapshot 404, and unknown-team 404 cases.
test_method: API route contract test plus Playwright League drill-in test
notes: Existing related test: tests/playwright/specs/league.spec.ts.

user_story: As a user, I want the deployed root page to load the current JS bundle so I do not get stuck on a stale frontend.
expected_behavior: The root endpoint reads index.html, replaces the app.js cache-bust token with a digest from the built bundle, and returns no-store headers; static files are served through NoCacheStaticFiles.
test_method: API response header/body test; Railway smoke after deploy
notes: Phase 1 inventory only; not tested in this loop yet.
